summary: Field Document Scanner (Phase 1) + FL hard pre-seal gate end-to-end testing: 12/12 scanner pytest green (incl. live GPT-5.2 Vision call), 5/5 FL pre-seal gate pytest green via direct DB seeding, 87/87 (8 skipped) M3/M4/M5/FL-compliance/KBA regression green, frontend /scanner full happy path green (capture → upload → submit → scanner-result → seal-btn → new-scan → recent-scans → row re-open). Mocked API: MockKBAProvider still in place; scanner AI uses live billable GPT-5.2 via EMERGENT_LLM_KEY.

minor.issue: First request from a fresh client occasionally exceeds 20s read timeout under load (single-shot retry added in test fixture). 5xx never observed via curl; appears transient. Not blocking.

ui_bugs.issue: When run_ai=false the result page still renders "MEDIUM RISK · MANUAL_REVIEW" + "AI degraded fallback — manual review recommended" because the UI treats null ai_analysis the same as a degraded fallback. Functionally correct but misleading to users who explicitly toggled AI off — consider a distinct empty-state copy like 'AI check skipped'.

issues: Console: 'Each child in a list should have a unique key prop' warning in Dashboard render (not scanner).

action_items: (LOW) Differentiate FieldScanner result-card copy when run_ai=false vs true-but-degraded — currently both show 'AI degraded fallback'.
(LOW) Investigate intermittent slow first-hit on /api/auth/login from cold pytest clients (curl is sub-second; likely TLS/proxy warmup).
critical_code_review_comments: field_scanner_routes.py — clean: Pydantic validates pages (min_length=1, max_length=20), excludes _id via projection, scopes list by user_id (admin sees all), 403 if not owner/admin, 404 for unknown — all behaviors verified by tests.

field_scanner_service.py — canonical_document_hash sorts page hashes before joining → idempotent regardless of page upload order (verified). _strip_data_url handles 'data:' prefix; analyze_pages_for_forgery uses LlmChat with file_contents=ImageContent(...) (correct emergentintegrations kwarg per agent context). check_existing_seal matches document_hash | message_hash | verification_hash with sealed_at DESC sort — correctly surfaces newest prior seal.

ceremony_routes.py FL hard pre-seal gate (lines 544-602) — correct: only runs when consensus=APPROVED, looks up fl_jurisdiction_qualifications by ceremony_id, computes block reasons (kba_not_passed / av_quality_not_reported / av_quality_not_passed / witnesses_short:{n}_of_2 for will|online_will), forces final_status='fl_blocked' and skips _seal_on_hedera when reasons non-empty, persists blockchain_seal=None + fl_blocked_reasons. Exception in the gate is swallowed and seal proceeds (defensive — flagged: this could allow a seal through if mongo throws transiently; consider failing closed for FL ceremonies).

Scanner seal endpoint correctly delegates to hedera_service.seal_document and persists the seal payload; idempotency check via existing s.sealed && s.seal short-circuits to already_sealed=True (verified).
Recommend: emergentintegrations API key is real/billable — consider rate-limiting POST /api/scanner/scans?run_ai=true per user to prevent runaway cost.

success_rate.backend: 100% (104/104 executed; 8 KBA tests skipped by design — same as iter100)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 7 scanner data-testid's + full happy path verified)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@notarychain.com / Admin123! ; User: demo@test.com / Demo123! (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md)
seed_data_creation: Inserted+cleaned TEST_-prefixed docs in fl_jurisdiction_qualifications, kba_attempts, fl_av_quality_reports, fl_will_witnesses, blockchain_seals during pytest. Real field_scans records persisted under demo@test.com (TEST_-prefixed labels; safe to leave or sweep by 'document_label' LIKE 'TEST_%').
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both Phase-1 Field Document Scanner and FL hard pre-seal gate are production-ready. Scanner: ai_powered=true & model='gpt-5.2' verified live. FL gate verified via DB-seeded fixture replaying the exact gate block from ceremony_routes.py (548-589) — full 3-agent ceremony pipeline run not exercised here (would require LLM fixtures); main agent should run one real online-will ceremony with missing KBA at least once to confirm end-to-end. Note dup-key indexes on fl_will_witnesses (witness_id_1, token_hash_1) require providing those fields when seeding.
rca of the issue: n/a — no blocking bugs found this iteration.
